speaker: BAPTISTA
text: Signior Lucentio, this is the ’pointed day

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: That Katherine and Petruchio should be married,

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: And yet we hear not of our son-in-law.

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: What will be said? What mockery will it be,

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: To want the bridegroom when the priest attends

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: To speak the ceremonial rites of marriage?

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: What says Lucentio to this shame of ours?

speaker: KATHERINE
text: No shame but mine. I must, forsooth, be forced

speaker: KATHERINE
text: To give my hand, opposed against my heart,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Unto a mad-brain rudesby, full of spleen,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Who wooed in haste and means to wed at leisure.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: I told you, I, he was a frantic fool,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Hiding his bitter jests in blunt behavior,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: And, to be noted for a merry man,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: He’ll woo a thousand, ’point the day of marriage,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Make friends, invite, and proclaim the banns,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Yet never means to wed where he hath wooed.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Now must the world point at poor Katherine

speaker: KATHERINE
text: And say “Lo, there is mad Petruchio’s wife,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: If it would please him come and marry her.”

speaker: TRANIO
text: Patience, good Katherine, and Baptista too.

speaker: TRANIO
text: Upon my life, Petruchio means but well,

speaker: TRANIO
text: Whatever fortune stays him from his word.

speaker: TRANIO
text: Though he be blunt, I know him passing wise;

speaker: TRANIO
text: Though he be merry, yet withal he’s honest.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Would Katherine had never seen him, though!

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: Go, girl. I cannot blame thee now to weep,

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: For such an injury would vex a very saint,

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: Much more a shrew of thy impatient humor.

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: Master, master, news! And such old

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: news as you never heard of!

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: Is it new and old too? How may that be?

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: Why, is it not news to hear of Petruchio’s

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: coming?

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: Is he come?

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: Why, no, sir.

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: What then?

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: He is coming.

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: When will he be here?

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: When he stands where I am, and sees you there.

speaker: TRANIO
text: But say, what to thine old news?

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: Why, Petruchio is coming in a new hat and

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: an old jerkin, a pair of old breeches thrice turned,

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: a pair of boots that have been candle-cases, one

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: buckled, another laced; an old rusty sword ta’en

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: out of the town armory, with a broken hilt, and

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: chapeless; with two broken points; his horse

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: hipped, with an old mothy saddle and stirrups of no

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: kindred, besides possessed with the glanders and

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: like to mose in the chine, troubled with the lampass,

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: infected with the fashions, full of windgalls,

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: sped with spavins, rayed with the yellows, past cure

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: of the fives, stark spoiled with the staggers, begnawn

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: with the bots, swayed in the back and shoulder-shotten,

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: near-legged before, and with a half-checked

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: bit and a headstall of sheep’s leather,

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: which, being restrained to keep him from stumbling,

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: hath been often burst, and now repaired with

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: knots; one girth six times pieced, and a woman’s

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: crupper of velour, which hath two letters for her

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: name fairly set down in studs, and here and there

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: pieced with packthread.

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: Who comes with him?

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: Oh, sir, his lackey, for all the world caparisoned

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: like the horse: with a linen stock on one leg

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: and a kersey boot-hose on the other, gartered with

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: a red and blue list; an old hat, and the humor of

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: forty fancies pricked in ’t for a feather. A monster,

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: a very monster in apparel, and not like a Christian

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: footboy or a gentleman’s lackey.

speaker: TRANIO
text: ’Tis some odd humor pricks him to this fashion,

speaker: TRANIO
text: Yet oftentimes he goes but mean-appareled.

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: I am glad he’s come, howsoe’er he comes.

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: Why, sir, he comes not.

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: Didst thou not say he comes?

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: Who? That Petruchio came?

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: Ay, that Petruchio came!

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: No, sir, I say his horse comes with him on

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: his back.

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: Why, that’s all one.

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: Nay, by Saint Jamy.

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: I hold you a penny,

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: A horse and a man

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: Is more than one,

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: And yet not many.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Come, where be these gallants? Who’s at home?

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: You are welcome, sir.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: And yet I come not well.

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: And yet you halt not.

speaker: TRANIO
text: Not so well appareled as I wish

speaker: TRANIO
text: you were.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Were it better I should rush in thus—

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: But where is Kate? Where is my lovely bride?

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: How does my father? Gentles, methinks you frown.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: And wherefore gaze this goodly company

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: As if they saw some wondrous monument,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Some comet or unusual prodigy?

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: Why, sir, you know this is your wedding day.

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: First were we sad, fearing you would not come,

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: Now sadder that you come so unprovided.

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: Fie, doff this habit, shame to your estate,

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: An eyesore to our solemn festival.

speaker: TRANIO
text: And tell us what occasion of import

speaker: TRANIO
text: Hath all so long detained you from your wife

speaker: TRANIO
text: And sent you hither so unlike yourself.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Tedious it were to tell, and harsh to hear.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Sufficeth I am come to keep my word,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Though in some part enforcèd to digress,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Which at more leisure I will so excuse

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: As you shall well be satisfied with all.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: But where is Kate? I stay too long from her.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: The morning wears. ’Tis time we were at church.

speaker: TRANIO
text: See not your bride in these unreverent robes.

speaker: TRANIO
text: Go to my chamber, put on clothes of mine.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Not I, believe me. Thus I’ll visit her.

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: But thus, I trust, you will not marry her.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Good sooth, even thus. Therefore, ha’ done with words.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: To me she’s married, not unto my clothes.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Could I repair what she will wear in me,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: As I can change these poor accoutrements,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: ’Twere well for Kate and better for myself.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: But what a fool am I to chat with you

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: When I should bid good morrow to my bride

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: And seal the title with a lovely kiss!

speaker: TRANIO
text: He hath some meaning in his mad attire.

speaker: TRANIO
text: We will persuade him, be it possible,

speaker: TRANIO
text: To put on better ere he go to church.

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: I’ll after him, and see the event of this.

speaker: TRANIO
text: But, sir, to love concerneth us to add

speaker: TRANIO
text: Her father’s liking, which to bring to pass,

speaker: TRANIO
text: As I before imparted to your Worship,

speaker: TRANIO
text: I am to get a man (whate’er he be

speaker: TRANIO
text: It skills not much, we’ll fit him to our turn),

speaker: TRANIO
text: And he shall be “Vincentio of Pisa,”

speaker: TRANIO
text: And make assurance here in Padua

speaker: TRANIO
text: Of greater sums than I have promisèd.

speaker: TRANIO
text: So shall you quietly enjoy your hope

speaker: TRANIO
text: And marry sweet Bianca with consent.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Were it not that my fellow schoolmaster

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Doth watch Bianca’s steps so narrowly,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: ’Twere good, methinks, to steal our marriage,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Which, once performed, let all the world say no,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: I’ll keep mine own despite of all the world.

speaker: TRANIO
text: That by degrees we mean to look into,

speaker: TRANIO
text: And watch our vantage in this business.

speaker: TRANIO
text: We’ll overreach the graybeard, Gremio,

speaker: TRANIO
text: The narrow prying father, Minola,

speaker: TRANIO
text: The quaint musician, amorous Litio,

speaker: TRANIO
text: All for my master’s sake, Lucentio.

speaker: TRANIO
text: Signior Gremio, came you from the church?

speaker: GREMIO
text: As willingly as e’er I came from school.

speaker: TRANIO
text: And is the bride and bridegroom coming home?

speaker: GREMIO
text: A bridegroom, say you? ’Tis a groom indeed,

speaker: GREMIO
text: A grumbling groom, and that the girl shall find.

speaker: TRANIO
text: Curster than she? Why, ’tis impossible.

speaker: GREMIO
text: Why, he’s a devil, a devil, a very fiend.

speaker: TRANIO
text: Why, she’s a devil, a devil, the devil’s dam.

speaker: GREMIO
text: Tut, she’s a lamb, a dove, a fool to him.

speaker: GREMIO
text: I’ll tell you, Sir Lucentio: when the priest

speaker: GREMIO
text: Should ask if Katherine should be his wife,

speaker: GREMIO
text: “Ay, by gog’s wouns!” quoth he, and swore so loud

speaker: GREMIO
text: That, all amazed, the priest let fall the book,

speaker: GREMIO
text: And as he stooped again to take it up,

speaker: GREMIO
text: This mad-brained bridegroom took him such a cuff

speaker: GREMIO
text: That down fell priest and book, and book and priest.

speaker: GREMIO
text: “Now, take them up,” quoth he, “if any list.”

speaker: TRANIO
text: What said the wench when he rose again?

speaker: GREMIO
text: Trembled and shook, for why he stamped and swore

speaker: GREMIO
text: As if the vicar meant to cozen him.

speaker: GREMIO
text: But after many ceremonies done,

speaker: GREMIO
text: He calls for wine. “A health!” quoth he, as if

speaker: GREMIO
text: He had been aboard, carousing to his mates

speaker: GREMIO
text: After a storm; quaffed off the muscatel

speaker: GREMIO
text: And threw the sops all in the sexton’s face,

speaker: GREMIO
text: Having no other reason

speaker: GREMIO
text: But that his beard grew thin and hungerly,

speaker: GREMIO
text: And seemed to ask him sops as he was drinking.

speaker: GREMIO
text: This done, he took the bride about the neck

speaker: GREMIO
text: And kissed her lips with such a clamorous smack

speaker: GREMIO
text: That at the parting all the church did echo.

speaker: GREMIO
text: And I, seeing this, came thence for very shame,

speaker: GREMIO
text: And after me I know the rout is coming.

speaker: GREMIO
text: Such a mad marriage never was before!

speaker: GREMIO
text: Hark, hark, I hear the minstrels play.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Gentlemen and friends, I thank you for your pains.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: I know you think to dine with me today

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: And have prepared great store of wedding cheer,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: But so it is, my haste doth call me hence,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: And therefore here I mean to take my leave.

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: Is ’t possible you will away tonight?

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: I must away today, before night come.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Make it no wonder. If you knew my business,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: You would entreat me rather go than stay.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: And, honest company, I thank you all,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: That have beheld me give away myself

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: To this most patient, sweet, and virtuous wife.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Dine with my father, drink a health to me,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: For I must hence, and farewell to you all.

speaker: TRANIO
text: Let us entreat you stay till after dinner.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: It may not be.

speaker: GREMIO
text: Let me entreat you.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: It cannot be.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Let me entreat you.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: I am content.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Are you content to stay?

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: I am content you shall entreat me stay,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: But yet not stay, entreat me how you can.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Now, if you love me, stay.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Grumio, my horse.

speaker: GRUMIO
text: Ay, sir, they be ready; the oats have eaten the

speaker: GRUMIO
text: horses.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Nay, then,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Do what thou canst, I will not go today,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: No, nor tomorrow, not till I please myself.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: The door is open, sir. There lies your way.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: You may be jogging whiles your boots are green.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: For me, I’ll not be gone till I please myself.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: ’Tis like you’ll prove a jolly surly groom,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: That take it on you at the first so roundly.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: O Kate, content thee. Prithee, be not angry.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: I will be angry. What hast thou to do?—

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Father, be quiet. He shall stay my leisure.

speaker: GREMIO
text: Ay, marry, sir, now it begins to work.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Gentlemen, forward to the bridal dinner.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: I see a woman may be made a fool

speaker: KATHERINE
text: If she had not a spirit to resist.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: They shall go forward, Kate, at thy command.—

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Obey the bride, you that attend on her.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Go to the feast, revel and domineer,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Carouse full measure to her maidenhead,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Be mad and merry, or go hang yourselves.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: But for my bonny Kate, she must with me.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Nay, look not big, nor stamp, nor stare, nor fret;

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: I will be master of what is mine own.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: She is my goods, my chattels; she is my house,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: My household stuff, my field, my barn,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: My horse, my ox, my ass, my anything.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: And here she stands, touch her whoever dare.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: I’ll bring mine action on the proudest he

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: That stops my way in Padua.—Grumio,

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Draw forth thy weapon. We are beset with thieves.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Rescue thy mistress if thou be a man!—

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: Fear not, sweet wench, they shall not touch thee, Kate.

speaker: PETRUCHIO
text: I’ll buckler thee against a million.

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: Nay, let them go. A couple of quiet ones!

speaker: GREMIO
text: Went they not quickly, I should die with laughing.

speaker: TRANIO
text: Of all mad matches never was the like.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Mistress, what’s your opinion of your sister?

speaker: BIANCA
text: That being mad herself, she’s madly mated.

speaker: GREMIO
text: I warrant him, Petruchio is Kated.

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: Neighbors and friends, though bride and bridegroom wants

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: For to supply the places at the table,

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: You know there wants no junkets at the feast.

speaker: BAPTISTA
text:
To
                 
                Tranio
                .
                 
              Lucentio, you shall supply the bridegroom’s place,

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: And let Bianca take her sister’s room.

speaker: TRANIO
text: Shall sweet Bianca practice how to bride it?

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: She shall, Lucentio. Come, gentlemen, let’s go.
